# CalPrep RE

> Free California real estate exam prep — study guides, a free diagnostic quiz, and the CalPrep RE iOS app with 1,900+ DRE-specific practice questions.

Facts on these pages are verified against primary sources (DRE / TREC / DBPR / Pearson VUE) and dated. Fee and format figures reflect the current schedules as of the date shown on each page.

## Study guides & exam facts

- [What to Bring to the California Real Estate Exam (Exam Day Checklist)](https://calprepre.com/exam-day.html): Bring two items: your barcoded Exam Schedule Notice printed from eLicensing and one valid photo ID — a current driver's license or DMV ID card, passport, or U.S. military ID (DRE accepts exactly those forms). Arrive 30 minutes early; exams are electronic at DRE exam centers in Fresno, La Palma, Oakland, Sacramento, and San Diego (not offered online). Prohibited items include phones, watches, keys, hats, food, and study materials — lockers or a storage table provided. Results appear on screen at completion (passers aren't told their score); missed or moved dates rebook via eLicensing for the $40 reschedule fee. Per DRE's official examinee instructions.
- [How Long Does It Take to Get a California Real Estate License?](https://calprepre.com/how-long.html): Most people: 3–6 months. DRE pacing rules require a minimum 18 days (2.5 weeks) per course — a hard ~54-day floor on the three required courses (135 hours), so no one can legally finish faster than about three months. The combo exam+license application ($450) avoids queueing twice; clean applications processed in roughly 2–3 weeks as of July 2026 (DRE updates timeframes weekly).
- [California Real Estate License Reciprocity: There's None — Here's the Out-of-State Path](https://calprepre.com/reciprocity.html): California has no real estate reciprocity with any state and waives no requirements (per DRE). Residency is NOT required; accredited out-of-state education can satisfy the three-course requirement; nonresidents file the notarized RE 234 Consent to Service of Process. Everyone takes the 150-question exam (70% to pass). No "license transfer" mechanism exists.
- [California Real Estate Exam Topics: Where to Focus Your Study Time](https://calprepre.com/exam-topics.html): One topic is 25% of the CA real estate exam. This California DRE study guide breaks down all 7 topic areas by weight so you know exactly where to focus.
- [Can You Bring a Calculator to the California Real Estate Exam?](https://calprepre.com/calculator.html): The DRE website contradicts itself on calculators. Here's the real answer — and what CA real estate exam math you actually need to know.
- [Does the California Real Estate Exam Have Math?](https://calprepre.com/math.html): Not exactly. The CA real estate exam won't make you crunch numbers — but math formulas absolutely appear. Here's what you actually need to know.
- [I Failed the California Real Estate Exam](https://calprepre.com/fail.html): Failed the California real estate exam? Here's what to do next — the retake process, the $100 fee, how your score report works, and the 2-year deadline to know about.
- [California Real Estate Exam Vocabulary: 50 Terms You Need to Know](https://calprepre.com/vocab.html): The 50 most-tested California real estate exam terms — from OLDCAR and Mello-Roos to non-judicial foreclosure — with plain-English, exam-focused definitions organized by DRE topic.
- [Free California Real Estate Practice Questions](https://calprepre.com/practice-questions.html): 20 free California real estate exam practice questions with answers and explanations — covering licensing, agency, contracts, disclosures, ownership, finance, and fair housing. No email required.
- [How Many Questions Is the California Real Estate Exam? Format, Time Limit & Passing Score](https://calprepre.com/exam-facts.html): The California real estate salesperson exam is 150 multiple-choice questions with a 3-hour time limit — you need 70% (105 correct) to pass. Full format, fees, scoring quirks, and retake rules.
- [How Much Does It Cost to Get a California Real Estate License? (2026 Fee Breakdown)](https://calprepre.com/cost.html): Expect $600–$900 all-in: $100–$400 for the required 135-hour courses, $100 DRE exam fee, $350 license fee, and about $70–80 for Live Scan fingerprinting. Full itemized fee table inside.

## Interactive

- [Free CA Real Estate Exam Diagnostic Quiz](https://calprepre.com/quiz.html): Take our free 14-question California real estate exam diagnostic quiz. Find your weak spots across all 7 DRE exam topics. No email required.
- [California Real Estate Exam Study Guides](https://calprepre.com/guides.html): Free study guides for the California real estate exam. DRE-specific, data-driven resources to help you pass on the first try.

## App

- [CalPrep RE on the App Store](https://calprepre.com/): iOS app with 1,900+ state-specific practice questions, flashcards, and timed exam simulations.
